firstAidSteps: Locate rescue inhaler (Albuterol) in backpack front pocket.
Assist patient in sitting upright and taking 2 puffs.
Call 911 if breathing doesn't improve within 5 minutes.

secureTier.clinicalNotes: Rising senior CS student. History of exercise-induced bronchospasms. High sensitivity to cold air and dust. Last hospitalized for asthma in 2024.

firstAidSteps: Patient is DEAF. Please use clear gestures or write notes on paper/phone.
Check for FAST stroke signs (Face drooping, Arm weakness, Speech difficulty).
If signs are present, write '911' on a paper and call immediately.

secureTier.clinicalNotes: History of ischemic stroke (2023). Uses American Sign Language (ASL) as primary communication. Left-sided mild residual weakness. Requires interpreter for medical consent.

firstAidSteps: Apply firm, continuous pressure to any bleeding site for at least 15 minutes.
DO NOT give aspirin or ibuprofen (causes increased bleeding).
Patient has factor VIII infusion kit in outdoor pack; assist if trained.

secureTier.clinicalNotes: Severe Hemophilia A. High risk of intracranial or joint bleeding after falls. Carry factor infusion instructions. Joint target is right knee.
